[[Darrell Griffin]] (brother) }} }} '''Josh Griffin''' (born 9 May 1990) is an English former [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[York Knights]] in the [[Super League]].{{cite news|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/main.jhtml?xml=/sport/2008/04/12/sorugl112.xml|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080413053048/http://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/main.jhtml?xml=/sport/2008/04/12/sorugl112.xml|url-status=dead|archive-date=2008-04-13|title=Wakefield forced to turn to youth|publisher=Daily Telegraph|date=11 April 2008|accessdate=2008-04-21|location=London|first=Ian|last=Laybourn}} He has previously played for [[Hull FC]], [[Huddersfield Giants]], [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Wakefield Trinity]] and the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]]. He spent time at the [[Batley Bulldogs]], on loan from [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] and also on permanent deal at [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Earlier in his career he played as a {{rlp|ce}} and {{rlp|wg}}er. Between 2012 and 2014 he played professional [[rugby union]] as a [[Rugby union positions#Wing|winger]] for [[Yorkshire Carnegie|Leeds Carnegie]] in the [[RFU Championship]]. ==Background== Griffin was born in [[Oxford]], England.{{citation needed|date=June 2023}} He then moved up to Wakefield with his family, and played his amateur rugby league for [[Stanley Rangers]] ==Club career== ===Wakefield Trinity Wildcats=== Having joined the club as a junior, Griffin made his professional début for Wakefield Trinity Wildcats in a [[Challenge Cup]] victory against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] on 20 April 2008.{{cite web|url=http://www.wakefieldwildcats.co.uk/salford-city-reds-8-38-wakefield-wildcats-i603768.html|title=Salford City Reds 8–38 Wakefield Trinity Wildcats|publisher=Wakefield Trinity Wildcats|date=21 April 2008|accessdate=2008-04-21|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110724110913/http://www.wakefieldwildcats.co.uk/salford-city-reds-8-38-wakefield-wildcats-i603768.html|archivedate=24 July 2011}} ===Huddersfield Giants=== He joined [[Huddersfield Giants]] at the end of the 2008 season. He made his début for Huddersfield in the [[Challenge Cup]] victory over [[Rochdale Hornets]] on 11 May 2009, and made his Super League début later that year.{{citation needed|date=March 2020}} Griffin spent most of 2010 on loan at [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]], and rejoined Wakefield Trinity Wildcats in 2011.{{citation needed|date=March 2020}} ===Castleford Tigers=== He signed for [[Castleford Tigers]] in September 2011, and played for them in 2012 before leaving the club to join rugby union club; [[Leeds Carnegie]] with immediate effect at the end of the season. ===Batley Bulldogs=== He returned to rugby league in 2014 with Batley.{{citation needed|date=March 2020}} ===Salford Red Devils=== In 2014, Griffin initially joined [[Salford Red Devils]] on a trial for the end of the 2014 season before securing himself a permanent two-year deal.{{citation needed|date=March 2020}} [[File:Josh Griffin.jpg|thumb|right|Griffin playing for the Salford Red Devils in 2015]] ===Hull F.C.=== In 2017, Griffin joined [[Hull F.C.]], and played in Hull's 18–14 [[2017 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]] final win over the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-final-live-score-11054626|title=Hull FC 18–14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs|publisher=Mirror|date=26 August 2017|accessdate=6 November 2019}} In the [[Super League XXV|2020 Super League season]], he made a total of 16 appearances. He played in Hull's 29–2 semi-final defeat against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]. In the first half, Griffin gave away a crucial penalty after Wigan had lost possession of the ball. Griffin raced over to Wigan player [[Oliver Partington]] and patted him on the head which sparked an all-in brawl. Wigan would then score two tries in quick succession from the resulting penalty.{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-hull-fc-highlights-4716172#964303/|title=Wigan Warriors v Hull FC highlights as FC struggle to find play-off form in one-sided defeat|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk}} In May 2021, Griffin scored his 100th career try in a match against the [[Leeds Rhinos]]. The match was Leeds' first home fixture in front of a home crowd for over 400 days as national restrictions were eased during the [[COVID-19 pandemic]].{{cite web |date=23 May 2021|title=Leeds 12–18 Hull: Visitors hold out in bruising encounter at sodden Headingley|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leeds-12-18-hull-visitors-hold-out-in-bruising-encounter-at-sodden-headingley/|access-date=2021-05-26|website=LoveRugbyLeague}} In the [[2021 Challenge Cup]] semi-final, Griffin ruptured his achilles during [[Hull FC]]'s 33–18 loss against [[St Helens RLFC]].{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-18-st-helens-33-josh-griffin-injury-horror-as-hull-fc-fall-short-in-challenge-cup-3262568/|title=Hull FC 18 St Helens 33: Josh Griffin injury horror as Hull FC fall short in Challenge Cup|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}} In round 14 of the 2023 Super League season, Griffin scored a [[hat-trick]] in Hull FC's upset victory over [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]].{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65754161|title=Super League: Hull FC 30–18 Warrington Wolves – Josh Griffin hat-trick seals comeback win|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} In the 2023 Challenge Cup quarter-final, Griffin was sent off for dissent just after half-time whistle in Hull FC's match against St Helens. St Helens would go on to win the match 32–18.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65953290#:~:text=Hull%20FC's%20Josh%20Griffin%20faces,argued%20with%20referee%20Chris%20Kendall./|title=Josh Griffin: Hull FC centre faces at least a six-game ban after red card in Challenge Cup loss|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} On 20 June 2023, Griffin was handed a seven-game ban by the RFL and fined £1000 over the incident.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65953290|title=Josh Griffin: Hull FC centre gets seven-game ban after red card in Challenge Cup loss|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} ===Wakefield Trinity=== In July 2023, Griffin signed a contract to join [[Wakefield Trinity]] which would last until the end of 2024.{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-hull-fc-man-josh-griffin-reveals-the-super-league-club-he-turned-down-to-sign-for-wakefield-trinity/|title=Ex-Hull FC man Josh Griffin reveals the Super League club he turned down to sign for Wakefield Trinity|website=www.totalrl.com}} Griffin played 20 games for Wakefield Trinity in the 2025 Super League season including their elimination play off loss against [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]].{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/rugby-league-news-what-next-for-wakefield-trinity-after-incredible-super-league-return-ends-in-play-off-loss-108937/|title=What next for Wakefield Trinity after incredible Super League return ends in play-off loss|website=www.seriousaboutrl.com}} ===York RLFC=== In August 2025, Griffin signed a contract to join [[York RLFC]] ahead of the 2026 season.{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/york-knights-sign-justin-sangare-to-continue-super-league-preparations-5358964/|title=York Knights sign Justin Sangaré to continue Super League preparations|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}} In June 2026, Griffin announced that he had retired from rugby league with immediate effect, after suffering a ruptured pectoral muscle. {{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/26160848.josh-griffin-retires-rugby-league-36-years-old/|title=Josh Griffin retires from rugby league at 36-years-old|website=www.yorkpress.co.uk/}} ==Personal life== Griffin is the younger brother of the rugby league player [[Darrell Griffin]],{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2008/apr/12/rugbyleague.huddersfieldgiants|title=Wakefield injury crisis offers Griffin brothers chance to face off for Yorkshire bragging rights|publisher=Guardian|date=12 April 2008|access-date=2008-04-21|location=London|first=Andy|last=Wilson}} and the older brother of [[George Griffin (rugby league)|George Griffin]]. All three brothers played together for Salford in 2015. ==Honours== ===Hull FC=== *[[Challenge Cup]]: (1) 2017 ===Wakefield Trinity=== *[[RFL 1895 Cup]] (1): 2024 *[[RFL Championship Leaders' Shield]]: 2024 ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://www.hullfc.com/team/first-team/josh-griffin Hull F.C. profile] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20160428011426/http://www.reddevilsrugby.co.uk/player/josh-griffin/ Salford profile] *[https://www.superleague.co.uk/player/432/josh%3Cbr-%3Egriffin SL profile] {{Hull F.C. - 2017 Challenge Cup Final winners}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Griffin, Josh}} [[Category:1990 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:21st-century English sportsmen]] [[Category:Batley Bulldogs players]] [[Category:Castleford Tigers players]] [[Category:English rugby league players]] [[Category:English rugby union players]] [[Category:Huddersfield Giants players]] [[Category:Hull F.C. players]] [[Category:Leeds Tykes players]] [[Category:Rugby league centres]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Oxfordshire]] [[Category:Rugby league wingers]] [[Category:Rugby union players from Oxford]] [[Category:Rugby union wings]] [[Category:Salford RLFC players]] [[Category:Sportspeople from Oxford]] [[Category:Wakefield Trinity players]] [[Category:York Knights players]]
